NetSparkleUpdater.​Interfaces.​IAssemblyAccessor
public interface
NetSparkleUpdater.​Interfaces.​IAssemblyAccessor
NetSparkle.​Tests.​AppCastGenerator.​FakeTestDataAssemblyAccessor
NetSparkleUnitTests.​FakeTestDataAssemblyAccessor
NetSparkleUpdater.​AssemblyAccessors.​AsmResolverAccessor
NetSparkleUpdater.​AssemblyAccessors.​AssemblyDiagnosticsAccessor
NetSparkleUpdater.​AssemblyAccessors.​AssemblyReflectionAccessor

An assembly accessor grabs details on the current version and publisher information for a C# application (presumably the one that is currently running). This is intended to be used in conjunction with a Configuration subclass in order to retrieve and store info on the latest version of the app that was used, whether the app has run 1x or not, etc.  These fields may or may not be required based on the specific Configuration that you use. At the very least, make sure your IAssemblyAccessor can grab valid info for the title of the application, the product name (which may or may not be the same as the title), and the version of the application (e.g. 3.1.2.0).

Properties
AssemblyCompany
string AssemblyCompany {
get
}

The publisher of the application.  From < Company > tag in .csproj file

AssemblyCopyright
string AssemblyCopyright {
get
}

The copyright for the application (e.g. © 2020) From < Copyright > tag in .csproj file

AssemblyDescription
string AssemblyDescription {
get
}

Description of the assembly.  From < Description > tag in .csproj file

AssemblyTitle
string AssemblyTitle {
get
}

Title of the assembly, e.g. "My Best Product" From < AssemblyTitle > tag in .csproj file.  NOTE: AssemblyDiagnosticsAccessor returns AssemblyProduct here!

AssemblyProduct
string AssemblyProduct {
get
}

Product for the assembly. Might be the same as the title, but also could be more specific than the title.  From < Product > tag in .csproj file

AssemblyVersion
string AssemblyVersion {
get
}

Version of the item that's running. E.g. 3.1.2.1 or 3.1.2.1-beta1.  From < Version > tag in .csproj file
